what_it_does: Records a noticeboard design confirmation that existing mark/observation and reply machinery already supports the concept of typed cards on nodes and member corrections, and proposes treating a card's current understanding as a fold over an immutable observation thread.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies this as a current noticeboard block in channel dragnet-development, linked as part_of board://item-389c8489
Title frames the post as a confirmation that the machinery for cards-as-observations and members feeding/correcting them already exists
Body states the mark system already allows many typed observations per addressed node and supports filtering all marks on a thing or all marks of a type
Body states the comment/note/reply machinery plus typed board edges already provide member feeding/correction, though not yet wired to dragnet cards
Body draws a parallel to decisions: state is composed from an immutable observation-thread rather than mutating the thing itself
Proposes applying the same pattern so a card's current understanding of a file is the fold of the original dragnet observation, member corrections, and later re-readings
standouts: Confirms the cards-as-observations and member-correction concepts are already covered by existing mark and reply machinery
Reframes a card's understanding as a living fold over an immutable observation/correction thread instead of a fixed snapshot
conventions: Uses noticeboard item YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses board:// URI addressing for item identity and links
Markdown body has a top-level heading followed by bullet exposition
concerns: Claims are presented as confirmed by agents but no supporting references, code links, or evidence are included in the file
Terminology such as 'fold' and 'mark-is-truth' is used without operational definition in this file
notes: A design confirmation note arguing that dragnet cards and member corrections are already supported by existing mark/reply/decision machinery and should be modeled as an accumulating fold over observations.
questions: What is the precise mapping between a 'card' and a 'mark' in the existing system?
Which specific components implement the mark system and the comment/note/reply machinery?
What does 'fold' mean operationally for computing a card's current understanding?
What is the content and role of the parent item board://item-389c8489?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to confirm that the machinery for cards-as-observations and member feeding exists; the file does exactly that and extends it with a fold-state proposal, so purpose and content align.
apparent_themes: Immutable observation threads as source of truth
State as a fold over typed observations and corrections
Living, accumulating understanding versus fixed snapshots
intention_signals: Validate a design intuition by showing it maps to already-proven mechanisms, and steer implementation toward deriving card understanding from an accumulating thread of observations and corrections.
novelty: Treats a card's current understanding of a file not as a stored snapshot but as a continuously folded composition of the original observation, corrections, and re-readings, mirroring the decision observation-thread pattern.
contribution: Records the design insight that typed observations (marks) and threaded member replies already satisfy the cards-and-feeding concept, and that card state can be derived as a fold over an immutable observation thread.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item confirming that existing mark and reply machinery already supports typed cards and member corrections on nodes, and proposing that a card's current file understanding be derived as a fold over its observation/correction thread.
intention_for_embedding: To validate and record a design pattern where dragnet cards are treated as accumulating typed observations rather than fixed snapshots.
